observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive undertake the following courses of action:
a. Execute an order to commence a one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s Property, plant and equipment account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. Direct General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office to conduct reconciliation of records of the PPE accounts maintained by their respective offices; and,
c. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the PPE accounts after complying to the disposal procedures provided under Section 267 of the Local Government Accounting Manual and Sections 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Memorandum Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: An executive order shall be issued to the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to conduct a one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the Municipality and conduct reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going.   Unserviceable properties have not been disposed yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Office that in the preparation of the RPCPPE, ensure: (a) The adaption of the classification of PPE accounts used in the accounting records; (b) That the condition of each property item during the physical count are indicated in the report to determine its serviceability and if disposal thereof is proper; and, (c) The inclusion of the Land, Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets and Buildings & Other Structures accounts.
We further recommended directing the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to establish an effective or practical system that would ensure the expeditious and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment taking into consideration the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to implement the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Relevant data were still not included in the RPCPPE. It was also submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the implementing office in the granting of loan programs to conduct a regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure collection when due and demandable.
Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the fund transfers as prescribed under Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
Finally, we also recommended the Municipal Mayor to file request for authority to write-off the dormant Due from NGO/POs account to COA duly supported by the documents prescribed under Section 8.0 of the same circular.
observations.managementAction:
The observation had already been brought to the attention of the Municipal Agriculturist. The list of the individuals who were granted the loans recorded in the Due from NGO/POs account have been prepared and subjected for actual verification by the Municipal Agriculturist Office.
The management submitted a request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following:
 a. Upon receipt of Auditor’s advice, to order the withholding of the payment of any money due the accountable officers who failed to liquidated or submit explanation after 30 days from demand by COA;
 b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances within the period prescribed by the laws, rules, and regulations in the grant and liquidations of cash advances; and,
 c. Direct the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as at December 31, 2020 to submit immediately the liquidations thereof to avoid elevation of the matter to the Office of the Ombudsman and Civil Services Commission for possible filing of criminal and/or administrative charges.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to issue demand letters and withhold monies due to accountable officers. They also committed to implement the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Unliquidated cash advances was still noted as December 31, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce settlement of outstanding balances of the disallowances as at December 31, 2020.

observations.reason: Most of them are no longer connected with the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:

 a) Ensure to include in the Inspectorate Team LGU personnel who are more knowledgeable in the field of water system projects to achieve an effective and efficient water system related projects.
 b) Compel the contractor to rectify/repair any defects of the project that contribute to the 100% non-operational of the project since this is still within its contract warranty period.
 c) Compel the contractor to speed up the delivery of a quality project or else recommend the blacklisting of the said contractor (after due process) from participating in any Public Bidding which the LGU will conduct in the future.
 b. Rehabilitation of Potable Water System – Poblacion Montevista, Compostela Valley Province – abandonment of the project.

 b. Compel the erring contractor to speed up the delivery of a quality project least it will be blacklisted in the future’s public bidding. Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation on the said observations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete documentation be attached to all fund transfers to barangays and to adhere strictly to the documentary requirements set forth under Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; in violation of Section 4(6) of PD 1445, thereby casting doubt on the propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Require or remind the Municipal Accountant on the foregoing COA recommendation concerning fund transfers to Barangays.
observations.reason: Incomplete supporting documents are still noted on some vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant review the information presented in the different financial statements and to make adjustment when necessary, to reconcile the amounts and accounts presented in the financial statements, failing which would render their financial statements as misrepresented which is not in conformance with Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Validate the identified reconciling items for preparation of necessary adjustments and journal entries to account the same in the books and make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the cash in bank balance and recognize the corresponding liability for the stale checks.
Ensure that all book reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements are adjusted at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the fund transfers to LGU in consonance with Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the implementing government entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of the balances, in conformance with Section 7.2 of COA Circular NO. 2016-005.
Fast track the issuance of demand letters to recipient LGUs for the immediate liquidation of the fund transfers, and henceforth require the recipient LGU to liquidate fund transfers within the prescribed period.
Provide the Office of the Auditor with a status report on the liquidation of the Due from LGU account.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and provide the corresponding depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation and submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking up the provision of the depreciation expense and the corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Hasten the gathering of the necessary documents required in the derecognition of the properties already turned-over to and accepted by the Barangays or other entities and secure an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan by way of resolution to authorize the Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation (DOD) or a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);
Draw a Journal Entry Voucher derecognizing the assets from the books of the Municipality and furnish the respective beneficiaries a copy of the JEV and MOA/DOD to facilitate the recording in the latter’s respective books; and
Provide the Office of the Auditor copies of the JEV together with the supporting documents taking up the derecognition of the above-cited PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims, ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents and drop from the books those accounts that were not supported with necessary documents.
revert payables aged more than two years to be without valid claims, pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documentations and not recognized those that are not duly supported. reconcile the noted variance between the total ending balances in the general ledger and the subsidiary ledgers as at December 31, 2021 and effect the necessary adjusting entries if warranted; and,
ensure regular reconciliation between the balances recorded in the general ledger and the subsidiary ledger to correct errors in the books at the earliest time possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Determine the nature of fund transfer to the five (5) Barangays whether it pertains to financial aid/assistance or for the implementation of projects and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting records if warranted.
Submit copies of the Memorandum of Agreements entered into by the Municipality of Montevista with the above-mentioned barangays for review.
Issue demand letters to the recipient Barangays to immediately liquidate their outstanding fund transfers.
Stop the practice of granting additional fund transfers to recipient barangays until and unless previous transfers have been liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
determine the status of implementation of the programs and projects pertaining to the fund transfers; and
immediately return the unexpended balance of the fund transfers to the source agency or Bureau of Treasury whichever is applicable.

We recommended that Management include in their budget discussion the long unexpended continuing appropriation balances and declare it as savings; and revert the balances to the unappropriated surplus of the 20% Development Fund and LDRRM Fund for it to be reprogrammed to another vital developmental and DRRM projects/programs/activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Office to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually in accordance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.
We also recommended that the MDRRM Office submits to the Office of the Auditor the LDDRM Financial Investment Plan for CY2021.

We recommended that the Management submit justification for the under/non- withholding of the Creditable Withholding Tax on the allowances and/or honorarium paid to judges, election officers, fire marshal, etc.
We likewise recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct the applicable withholding tax on allowances, honorarium, and similar payments subject to withholding tax as required under Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting Office to immediately remit the balances of taxes/contributions/loan payments withheld from prior years and adhere strictly with the concerned agencies’ regulations on the prescribed period of remittances to avoid payment of interests, penalties and surcharges; prevent any undue disadvantages and inconvenience on the part of the employees upon availment of their benefits; and to allow the use of the concerned agencies collections for government projects.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the 442 JEVs including its supporting documents to COA and ensure that financial reports and documents are submitted within the reglementary period.
We further recommended the setting-up of cut-off date at year-end on the processing and submission of claims to the Municipal Accounting Office to avoid the influx of transactions at year-end which could overwhelm and affect the timely completion and submission of financial reports and its supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to submit written justification for the incurrence of delay in the implementation of the Farm to Market Road with installation of Solar Lights located at New Calape, Montevista, Davao de Oro and apprise the audit team of the action taken by management.
We further recommended that the management improve its project monitoring in the implementation of its infrastructure projects and effect immediate action to the delays incurred to ensure the economy and efficiency in the delivery of basic services and facilities to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the Annual and Supplemental Annual Investment Programs of the Municipality be formally endorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan prior its approval.
observations.managementAction: They also explained that due to the health restriction imposed due to COVID-19 pandemic, the Council was not able to convene for the approval of the Supplemental Annual Investment Programs. They committed that the deficiencies shall addressed in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to formally explain the non-compliance to Section 4.2 of the Appropriation Ordinance and to consult with the Sangguniang Bayan of the true intention of the said special provision.

However, they agreed that the way the provision was presented in the ordinance, the interpretation of the Audit Team can be valid. Thus, they refered the matter to the Sangguniang Bayan for their proper action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Office to include the specific LDRRM PPA’s presented in the LDRRMFIP including the expenditure accounts per PPA in the Annual and Supplemental Budgets to be enacted through an Appropriation Ordinance.
We further recommended the submission of the specific authorization from the Sanggunian Bayan to the Local Chief Executive to enter into contract for the implementation of LDDRM PPAs presented in the LDRRMFIP CY2020; if no authorization was made, to secure the same before the implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDDRM Officer and the Municipal Budget Office to include all estimated income from regular sources in the computation of the minimum 5% allocation of LDRRM Fund as prescribed in Section 21 of Republic Act (RA) No. 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Secretariat and General Services Office in coordination with other concerned offices of the Municipality to ensure the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to COA within five (5) working days upon its execution by designating the responsible office to do the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following:
Upon receipt of Auditor’s advice, to order the withholding of the payment of any money due the accountable officers who failed to liquidated or submit explanation after 30 days from demand by COA;
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances within the period prescribed by the laws, rules, and regulations in the grant and liquidations of cash advances; and,
Direct the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as at December 31, 2020 to submit immediately the liquidations thereof to avoid elevation of the matter to the Office of the Ombudsman and Civil Services Commission for possible filing of criminal and/or administrative charges.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce settlement of outstanding balances of the disallowances as at December 31, 2020.

We recommended to the management that Programs/Projects/Activities reflected in the Approved Annual and Supplemental Budgets for LDRRMF shall be itemized and not through lumpsum appropriation. Further, consider reviewing the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the inconsistency in the total amount shown for calamity fund as against the approved budget.
observations.managementAction:

Further, consider reviewing the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the inconsistency in the total amount shown for calamity fund as against the approved budget.
(b) Observe the desired template opined by COA in pursuance with Section 4.0 of NDRRMC-DBM DILG JMC No. 2013-1 Classifying the 70% CF into four (4) Thematic areas.
observations.reason: This audit observation have been reiterated in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation / s:

    We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to:
    1. To compute and recognize depreciation expense  for  Agricultural  and
       Forestry;


    2. Reconcile the discrepancies  between  the  consolidated  balances  of
       accounts of Subsidy from Other Funds and Subsidy to Other Funds;

    3.  Prepare  necessary  adjusting  entries  to  correct  the   erroneous
       classification of Social Pension for Indigent Senior  Citizens  under
       Pension Benefits account; and,

    4. Observe the requirement under PPSAS 1,  paragraph  127  (c)  and  COA
       Circular 2015-008 dated  November  23,  2015  on  the  disclosure  of
       additional information that is not shown on the face of the financial
       statements but relevant to the understanding of the users thereof.

Recommendation / s:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the “Inventory Committee”
to periodically conduct  a  physical  count  of  all  PPE  and  ensure  that
balances of the PPEs in  the  property  cards  maintained  by  the  GSO  are
reconciled with the ledger cards maintained by  the  MACCO.  Thereafter,  it
shall be required to submit the RPCPPE, duly signed  and  certified  correct
by the “Inventory Committee”, to the Office of the Audit Team Leader  within
the prescribed deadlines.

Recommendation / s:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the Municipal  Accounting
Office to  disclose  the  current  status  of  the  loans  and  present  the
individual  loan  balances  of  each  Promissory   Note,   issued   by   the
Municipality,  pertaining  to  its   Loans   Payable-Domestic   account   in
accordance with PPSAS 1, paragraph 127 (c).

Recommendation / s:


      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accounting
Office, in coordination with the  Municipal  Engineering  Office  and  other
concerned departments, to determine the status of the implementation of  the
programs  and  projects  pertaining  to  fund  transfers   with   non-moving
balances. For completed  programs  and  projects,  the  Municipality  should
return the unutilized balances to the  source  agency  or  to  the  National
Treasury. For unimplemented programs and projects, the Offices in charge  of
the implementation should determine the cause of  the  delay  and  formulate
alternative action plan to fast track the implementation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct MENR Office  to  ensure
strict implementation of  the  mandatory  segregation  at  source,  the  “No
Segregation, No Collection” policy of the Municipality, and  the  imposition
of fines  and  penalties  to  individuals  or  establishments  in  violation
thereof as provided under the existing Municipal Ordinances.
      Likewise, we recommended a permanent assignment of  personnel  at  the
Residual Containment Area (RCA)  to  properly  maintain  the  conversion  of
recyclable wastes with no delays.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the Municipal  Accounting
Office (MACCO) to transfer immediately the unexpended 30% QRF and 70%  MDRRM
Fund – MOOE to the Trust Fund books for the year 2019 and  do  the  same  at
year-end for the years thereafter in  compliance  with  the  accounting  and
reporting guidelines as prescribed under COA  Circular  No.  2012-02,  dated
September 12, 2012 and Section 21 of RA No. 10121.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor  to  direct  the  Municipal  Budget
Office to ensure that the LDRRM  PPAs’  names/descriptions  and/or  location
are duly specified and categorized into the four (4) thematic areas  in  the
approved Annual/Supplemental Budget.


      We further recommended the submission of  the  specific  authorization
from the Sanggunian Bayan  to  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to  enter  into
contract for the implementation of projects whose appropriation  are  stated
in generic terms in the approved Annual  and  Supplemental  Budgets;  if  no
authorization was made, to secure the same before the implementation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin  the  MDRRMO  to  prepare
annually the LDRRMFIP and use the  format  prescribed  in  Annex  A  of  COA
Circular     2012-002     and     to     ensure     the     inclusion     of
projects/programs/activities chargeable against the  unexpended  balance  of
MDRRM Fund therein to facilitate transparency  in  the  utilization  of  the
funds.

We recommended to  the  Municipal  Mayor  to  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Officer
(LDRRMO) to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of  LDRRM  Fund  as
of December 31,  2019  and  to  submit  the  same  on  a  monthly  basis  as
prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin  the  Municipal  Budget
Office to ensure that sufficient details of the  project  and  cost  in  the
succeeding appropriation ordinances are provided, most  especially  projects
funded under 20% Development Fund.


      We further recommended the submission of  the  specific  authorization
from the Sanggunian Bayan  to  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to  enter  into
contract for the implementation of projects whose appropriation  are  stated
in generic terms in the approved Annual  and  Supplemental  Budgets;  if  no
authorization was made, to secure the same before the implementation of  the
specific projects.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:


   1) To enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation
      of outstanding cash advances within the period prescribed by the laws,
      rules, and regulations in the grant and liquidations of cash advances;


   2) To stop the granting of cash advances  to  accountable  officers  with
      unliquidated cash advances; and,


   3) To direct the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances  as
      at December 31, 2019 to submit immediately the liquidations thereof to
      avoid elevation of  the  matter  to  the  Office  of the Ombudsman and Civil
      Services  Commission  for   possible   filing   of   criminal   and/or
      administrative charges.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor  to  enjoin  the  Municipal  Budget
Office to release in full the allotment for the appropriated amount for  20%
Development Fund to ensure the implementation of the developmental  priority
projects/programs/activities   through   an   efficient    and    economical
utilization of funds.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor  to  enjoin  the  Municipal  Budget
Office to release in full the allotment for the appropriated amount  for  5%
Calamity  Fund  to  ensure  the  implementation   of   the   DRRM   priority
projects/programs/activities and to lessen,  if  not  avoid,  the  risk  for
misusage of the fund.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Project-in-Charge  to
submit  written  justification  for  the  incurrence   of   delay   in   the
implementation  of  the  infrastructure  projects  funded  by  the  National
Government Agencies through fund transfer to the LGU.

      We further recommended that the management ensure  the  implementation
and completion of the infrastructure project within the year of the  receipt
of funds from the National Government Agencies and within  the  fiscal  year
it was budgeted to  avoid  possible  forfeiture  of  the  funding  from  the
National Government  upon  full  implementation  of  the  Annual  Cash-Based
Budgeting and to ensure the economy and efficiency of  the  Municipality  in
the delivery of basic services and facilities to its constituents.

We recommended to the management the following:
a) Integrate into the financial reports the qualitative characteristics such as reliability and comparability, in order to be useful to a wide range of decision-makers;
b) Provide only relevant but sufficient information, may it be narrative and descriptive and/or corroborating schedules in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
c) Ensure that the submitted reports are free from substantial inconsistencies that undermine the reliability of information.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

      We recommend  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to ensure that submission of the monthly reports be  on  time  as
prescribed under COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18,  1995,  COA  Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 122 of P.D. 1445 in  order  for
us to have a timely review of the financial transactions of your LGU.

      We also recommend to impose the suspension  of  the  salaries  of  the
personnel at the Municipal Accounting Office who  are  directly  involve  in
the submission of financial reports as this has  been  a  recurring  problem
every year, until they have  complied  with  the  requirements  as  mandated
under Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

Recommendation:

      We recommend  to  require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  prepare  an
analysis sheet for the reconciliation of the  Intra-Agency  Receivables  and
Payables accounts, to support the journal entry voucher to be drawn for  the
necessary adjustment to be made, in order to present  the  accurate  balance
of the aforementioned accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to
adhere strictly to  the  provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  2013-004  dated
January 30, 2013 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation  of
the projects.

      Likewise, to strictly comply with the required submission of  list  at
the prescribed format on PPAs  every  beginning  of  the  year  which  shall
include those at the planning  stage  and  those  on-going  projects.   This
requirement must be complied strictly  to  avoid  the  possibility  of  each
disbursement being questioned in audit, at the same  time  adhere  with  the
required transparency in the implementation of PPAs.

Recommendations:

      We recommend the management to strictly enforce  liquidation  of  cash
advances within the prescribed period as provided in COA  Circular  No.  97-
002.

      We also recommend withholding of salaries of the Accountable  Officers
still  employed  in  the  municipality  who  failed   to   liquidate   their
outstanding cash advances until the full amount is settled/liquidated.

Recommendations:


      We recommend to the management the following:

        the  immediate   settlement/liquidation   of   the   financial/cash
        assistance granted to the above-mentioned recipient  barangays  and
        NGOs/POs; b) to draw journal entry voucher  (JEV)  to  correct  the
        misclassified/and or erroneous booking of  the  released  financial
        assistance which involves the accounts  Due  from  LGUs,  Due  from
        NGOs/POs; c). to  verify  and  reconcile  with  the  books  of  the
        barangays as  to  the  entries  made  upon  receipt  of  the  funds
        transferred to them.

     2. To stop  granting  financial  assistance  to  barangays  and  other

        balances.
